textSnippets: We demonstrated that HCW9206, a unique protein scaffold linking interleukin-7 (IL-7), an IL-15/IL-15 receptor α (IL-15Rα) complex, and IL-21, generates CAR T cells without requiring αCD3/28 activation, which are highly enriched in long-lived T memory stem cells (TSCM cells) (>50%) and display potent activity across distinct disease models, HIV-1 or B cell leukemia.
In a humanized mouse HIV infection model, HCW9206-generated anti-HIV duoCAR T cells suppressed viremia more effectively than αCD3/28-generated anti-HIV duoCAR T cells.

In a xenograft leukemia mouse model, a recall proliferative response and complete clearance of leukemia rechallenge were displayed by HCW9206-generated but not by αCD3/28-generated antiCD19 CAR T cells.

HCW9206, a first-in-class cytokine scaffold–based platform, enables production of more potent CAR T cell–based immunotherapies by generating a CAR T cell population, which is highly functional and also markedly enriched for long-lived TSCM cells.

Together, these data demonstrate that treating T cells with a combination of IL-7, IL-15SA, and IL-21 cytokines delivered by a single biologic, HCW9206, can quickly and effectively produce HIVand CD19-specific CAR T cells that are highly enriched for the TSCM cell memory phenotype, as well as effector T cells capable of maintaining suppression of HIV and leukemic cell proliferation.

Therefore, generating CAR T cells through HCW9206 treatment could provide a new, improved, and highly scalable method for generating CAR T cells to treat patients with cancer and infectious diseases and replace standard CAR T cell production using αCD3/28 activation.
This has widespread implications for the generation of more robust CAR T cell–based immunotherapies to improve CAR T cell functional persistence and efficacy for the treatment of HIV and cancer.

With or without stimulation with 293T-gp120 cells, duoCAR THCW9206 cells exhibited distinct transcriptional differences as compared to duoCAR TαCD3/28 cells (fig.

In the comparison of duoCAR THCW9206 and duoCAR TαCD3/28 cells with or without stimulation with gp120, we identified 311 unique differentially expressed genes (DEGs) in the absence of gp120 activation, 172 DEGs after gp120 activation, and 128 shared DEGs (Fig.

These results indicate that the largest transcriptional differences between duoCAR THCW9206 and duoCAR TαCD3/28 cells are observed in unactivated CAR T cells; however, upon activation, their transcriptional profiles converge with lower amounts of DEGs, indicating an overlapping response to CAR stimulation by gp120.

Specifically, we found that unactivated CAR THCW9206 cells displayed an up-regulation of cytokine signaling– and JAK/STAT signaling–associated gene transcripts as compared to unactivated CAR TαCD3/28 cells, including STAT3, IL15RA, IL-2RA, IL6R, SOCS1, SOCS2, SOCS3, FOS, and JUN (fig.

IPA analysis comparing the transcriptomes of unactivated CAR THCW9206 cells and CAR TαCD3/28 cells identified enrichment in CAR THCW9206 cells for expression of genes associated with several pathways, including “cell viability,” “cell survival,” “cell movement,” and “stimulation of T lymphocytes” in the CAR THCW9206 cells (Fig.

The enrichment for expression of genes involved in cell viability and survival pathways in CAR THCW9206 cells, compared to CAR TαCD3/28 cells, indicates an increased fraction of CAR THCW9206 cells representing long-lived early memory TSCM cells.

In addition, comparing the transcriptomes of activated CAR THCW9206 cells and CAR TαCD3/28 cells revealed increased expression of genes in the CAR THCW9206 cells related to effector function pathways, including “cytotoxicity” and “internalization of cells,” as well as migratory pathways such as “extravasation of cells” (fig.

These results indicate that the transcriptome of CAR THCW9206 cells, compared to that of CAR TαCD3/28 cells, is enriched for cells with a prosurvival, TSCM-like transcriptome while retaining a population with a robust effector T cell transcriptome.

One study used isolated TN cells activated with αCD3/28 and cultured with IL-7 and IL-15 to generate CD19 CAR T cells enriched in the TSCM cell subset (27).

Together, these results indicate that CAR T cell production using HCW9206 synergizes the effects of IL-7, IL-15SA, and IL-21 to promote the generation of a CAR T cell product with a diverse mix of T cell subsets, which exhibit a combination of TSCM cell self-renewal capacity and enhanced T cell effector function, likely from the TEM cell population, indicating that CAR THCW9206 cells may be a more effective and long-lasting CAR T cell immunotherapy than conventional CAR TαCD3/28 cells.

In a humanized mouse model of HIV infection, we demonstrated that while both intravenously injected CAR T cell types could localize to the site of HIV infection in the mouse spleen at comparable levels, anti-HIV duoCAR THCW9206 cells demonstrated superior suppression of viremia compared to duoCAR TαCD3/28 cells (Fig.

Specifically, we demonstrated that in the mouse spleens injected with HIV-infected human PBMCs, the duoCAR THCW9206 (92.4%) cells showed significantly increased suppression (P = 0.0173) of HIV infection compared to duoCAR TαCD3/28 cells (49.7%).

This response was associated with a corresponding marked increase in CD19 CAR T cell expansion in the blood, spleen, and bone marrow of CD19 CAR THCW9206 cell–treated mice compared to those treated with CD19 CAR TαCD3/28 cells (Fig.
This correlated with the maintenance of circulating CD19 CAR TSCM cells we observed 44 days after the mice were injected with CD19 CAR THCW9206 cells but not with CD19 CAR TαCD3/28 cells (Fig.

TSCM cell enrichment in CAR T cells stimulated with HCW9206 represents a alternative strategy for generating CAR T cells, potentially providing treated patients with more prolonged and effective protection against cancer relapses.

Therefore, generating CAR T cells through HCW9206 treatment could provide a new, improved, and highly scalable method for generating CAR T cells to treat patients with cancer and infectious diseases and replace standard CAR T cell production using αCD3/28 activation.

sectionMap.title: Abstract
sectionMap.summary: We demonstrated that HCW9206, a unique protein scaffold linking interleukin-7 (IL-7), an IL-15/IL-15 receptor α (IL-15Rα) complex, and IL-21, generates CAR T cells without requiring αCD3/28 activation, which are highly enriched in long-lived T memory stem cells (TSCM cells) (>50%) and display potent activity across distinct disease models, HIV-1 or B cell leukemia.

sectionMap.title: Methods
sectionMap.summary: Together, these data demonstrate that treating T cells with a combination of IL-7, IL-15SA, and IL-21 cytokines delivered by a single biologic, HCW9206, can quickly and effectively produce HIVand CD19-specific CAR T cells that are highly enriched for the TSCM cell memory phenotype, as well as effector T cells capable of maintaining suppression of HIV and leukemic cell proliferation.
